content.content:
---
title: "Protocol 2026-W01: Base Building"
date: 2026-02-24
week_number: W01
tags: ["protocol"]
phase: "Base Building"
focus: "Swim Technique, Bike Form, Run Maintenance, Strength Preservation"
target_compliance: 85%
---

## Weekly Schedule

| Day | Type | Workout |
|-----|------|---------|
| Monday | Swim | 800m swim with technique drills: 4x100m freestyle (focus on breathing and form), 4x100m kickboard, 4x100m pull buoy |
| Tuesday | Strength | Workout A: Close grip bench press 3x8 @ 115 lbs, Cable seated row 3x11 @ 120 lbs, Barbell shoulder press 3x7 @ 85 lbs, Assisted pull up 3x2 @ 50 lbs, Dumbbell lying tricep extension 3x9 @ 20 lbs, Dumbbell incline curl 3x8 @ 20 lbs, Walking lunges 3x8 @ 20 lbs, Lying leg curl 3x10 @ 125 lbs |
| Wednesday | Bike | 60min indoor trainer: 20min warm-up, 30min at 85-95rpm, 10min cool-down |
| Thursday | Run | 4mi easy run (HR <150) |
| Friday | Strength | Workout B: Dumbbell bench press 3x10 @ 65 lbs, Dumbbell rows 3x11 @ 75 lbs, Cable tricep push downs 3x10 @ 140 lbs, Cable bicep curl 3x9 @ 100 lbs, Dumbbell overhead lateral raise 3x10 @ 12 lbs, Machine seated leg press 3x10 @ 330 lbs, Lying hamstring curls 3x9 @ 130 lbs |
| Saturday | Brick | 30min bike + 20min run (low intensity, focus on transitions) |
| Sunday | Rest | Active recovery: 30min walk, stretching, foam rolling |

## Training Goals
- Establish a consistent swim routine focusing on technique
- Build a solid base on the bike with proper form and cadence
- Maintain running volume while keeping intensity low
- Preserve full body strength with 2x/week full body workouts

## Weekly Targets

**Cardio Volume:**
- Swim: 800m
- Bike: 60 minutes
- Run: 4 miles

**Strength:**
- Increase reps on cable bicep curl and cable tricep push downs by 1 rep per set
- Maintain current weights on other lifts, focus on form and progressive overload

## AI Rationale

This protocol is designed to help Ryan transition smoothly from marathon running to triathlon training while preserving his strength. Given that he is a beginner in swimming and cycling, the initial focus is on technique and form to prevent injuries and build a strong foundation. The swim session is kept short and focused on drills to ensure he masters the basics. The bike session is also moderate, emphasizing form and cadence to build endurance gradually. Running is maintained at a low intensity to leverage his existing base without overloading his body.

Strength training is crucial to prevent muscle loss and support overall fitness. By alternating between two full-body workouts, Ryan can maintain balanced development and ensure progressive overload. The inclusion of a brick workout on Saturday helps him practice transitions, which is essential for triathlon performance.

The rest day on Sunday ensures adequate recovery, which is critical for sustained high performance. This balanced approach aligns with Ryan's goal of achieving top 0.01% performance across multiple domains while managing his startup and content creation responsibilities.

content.content:
---
title: "Protocol 2026-W07: Volume Building"
date: 2026-02-16
week_number: W07
tags: ["protocol"]
phase: "Volume Building"
focus: "Hypertrophy + Triathlon Base"
target_compliance: 85%
---

## Training Philosophy
- All sets to **1 rep in reserve (1 RIR)** — stop when you could do one more
- **Supersets** where marked to keep sessions tight
- Progression rule: if first set hits 12+ reps at 1 RIR, add 5 lbs next week
- Log reps as you go — no fixed rep targets, just weight and effort

## Weekly Schedule

| Day | Type | Workout |
|-----|------|---------|
| Monday | Swim | 400-800m freestyle (scale to energy) |
| Tuesday | Strength A | See below |
| Wednesday | Bike | 45 min indoor trainer (85-95 RPM, HR <150) |
| Thursday | Run | 4 miles easy (HR <150) |
| Friday | Strength B | See below |
| Saturday | Brick | 30 min bike + 20 min run |
| Sunday | Active Recovery | 30 min light swim or walk + stretching |

## Strength A (Tuesday)

Based on Feb 10 actuals with progression applied.

| Exercise | Weight | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Incline bench press | **120 lbs** | Up from 115. Hit 17/9/7 last week |
| Cable seated row | **145 lbs** | Up from 140. Dominated at 140 (20/11/11) |
| Overhead press | **85 lbs** | Hold weight. Aim for more consistency across sets (was 10/7/3) |
| Assisted chin-ups | **70 lbs assist** | Down from 75 assist. Hit 4/3/2 last week |
| Dumbbell lying tricep extension | 20 lbs | No recent data — hold and log |
| Dumbbell incline curl | 20 lbs | No recent data — hold and log |
| Walking lunges | **25 lbs** | Up from 20. Hit 12/10/7 last week |
| Lying hamstring curls | **130 lbs** | Up from 125. Nearly hit 10/10/10 |

**Supersets (suggested):**
- A1: Incline bench / A2: Cable seated row
- B1: Overhead press / B2: Assisted chin-ups
- C1: Tricep extension / C2: Incline curl
- D1: Walking lunges / D2: Hamstring curls

## Strength B (Friday)

Based on Feb 6 actuals (partial session) + Feb 15 (completed, no logged details). Progression conservative since data is limited.

| Exercise | Weight | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Dumbbell bench press | **65 lbs** | Up from 60. Hit 13/11/8 on Feb 6 |
| Dumbbell rows | **75 lbs** | Up from 70. Hit 13/12/7 on Feb 6 |
| Cable tricep push downs | 140 lbs | Hold — no recent rep data |
| Cable bicep curl | 100 lbs | Hold — no recent rep data |
| Dumbbell overhead lateral raise | **12 lbs** | Up from 10. Hit 14/10/8 on Feb 6 |
| Machine seated leg press | **330 lbs** | Up from 320. No recent data but conservative bump |
| Lying hamstring curls | **130 lbs** | Match Strength A progression |

**Supersets (suggested):**
- A1: DB bench press / A2: DB rows
- B1: Tricep push downs / B2: Bicep curls
- C1: Lateral raise / C2: (rest or core work)
- D1: Leg press / D2: Hamstring curls

## Volume Progression Plan

| Week | Sets per exercise | Goal |
|------|-------------------|------|
| W07 (this week) | 3 sets | Establish baseline at new weights |
| W08 | 3 sets | Push reps higher at same weight |
| W09 | 4 sets | Add volume |
| W10 | 4 sets | Push weight if reps are high |
| W11 | Deload | Drop to 2 sets, reduce weight 10% |

## Cardio Targets
- Swim: 400-800m (based on energy)
- Bike: 45 minutes
- Run: 4 miles
- Brick: 30+20 min

## Key Rules
- Log every set (weight x reps) — this is how we progress
- Wear the Garmin for every cardio session
- If gym is packed (Fridays), go earlier or swap to Saturday

input.title: 80/20 Half Ironman Training Protocol - Ryan's Build
input.content:
## 80/20 Half Ironman Protocol for Ryan

### Training Philosophy
- **80% easy intensity**: Aerobic base building at conversational pace
- **20% hard intensity**: High-intensity intervals, tempo work, race-pace efforts
- **Polarized structure**: Avoid "grey zone" moderate intensity
- **Progressive periodization**: 16-week build to Half Ironman (Oct 2026)

### Current Baseline (as of Mar 2, 2026)
- Weight: 256-258 lbs
- Swim: 400-800m base
- Bike: 45min indoor trainer base
- Run: 4 miles easy base
- Strength: 2x/week full-body (maintaining, not building)
- Target event: Half Ironman Oct 2026

### Target Weekly Volume by Phase
**Phase 1 (Base Building, Wk 1-4, Mar 2-30):**
- Swim: 3-4km/week (3 sessions)
- Bike: 4-5 hours/week (2-3 sessions)
- Run: 12-16 km/week (3 sessions)
- Strength: 1-2x/week (maintenance only during build)

**Phase 2 (Build, Wk 5-12, Apr-May):**
- Swim: 4-6km/week
- Bike: 6-8 hours/week
- Run: 16-20 km/week
- Race-pace/threshold work introduced

**Phase 3 (Peak, Wk 13-15, Jun-Sep):**
- Full race-distance simulation
- Long course work: Swim 4k, Bike 90min, Run 21k

### Intensity Distribution Rules
- Easy = <75% max HR, conversational pace
- Tempo/Threshold = 80-90% max HR
- High intensity/VO2 = 90%+ max HR
- **Goal: 80% of workouts easy, 20% hard (no grey zone)**

### Workout Template Structure
Each week follows:
- **Monday**: OFF or active recovery swim
- **Tuesday**: Hard bike (intervals/threshold) + strength
- **Wednesday**: Easy run
- **Thursday**: Hard run (intervals/tempo) + strength
- **Friday**: OFF or easy swim
- **Saturday**: Long bike + brick run (weekend long effort)
- **Sunday**: Long run OR recovery day

### Key Metrics to Track
- Pace/watts by heart rate zone
- Reps/weights for strength (maintenance focus)
- Weekly TSS (Training Stress Score)
- Sleep quality and recovery status

content.text: Good—I've captured the 80/20 framework in your brain. Now let me create THIS WEEK's protocol (Mar 2–8, W10) based on 80/20:

input.content:
---
title: "Protocol 2026-W10: 80/20 Base Building - Week 1"
date: 2026-03-02
week_number: W10
tags: ["protocol", "80/20", "triathlon"]
phase: "Base Building (80/20 Polarized)"
focus: "Aerobic Base + Easy Swim Technique, introduce high-intensity bike, maintain strength"
target_compliance: 85%
training_methodology: "80/20 Polarized Intensity Distribution"
---

## Weekly Philosophy
- **80% Easy**: Conversational pace, <75% max HR, focus on technique and aerobic base
- **20% Hard**: High-intensity intervals, tempo work, race-pace efforts
- **NO grey zone**: All workouts are clearly easy or clearly hard
- **Goal**: Build aerobic capacity while maintaining strength and introducing race-pace efforts

## Weekly Schedule

| Day | Type | Intensity | Workout | Duration |
|-----|------|-----------|---------|----------|
| **Monday** | Swim | Easy (80%) | 1000m easy freestyle: 200m warm-up + 6x100m easy (rest 20s between) + 200m cool-down. Focus on breathing rhythm and form. | 30 min |
| **Tuesday** | Bike + Strength | Hard (20%) | **Bike**: 10min warm-up + 4x4min hard intervals (threshold, 85-90% HR) at 90-95 RPM + 10min cool-down. **Then**: Strength A (see below) | 45 min bike + 35 min strength |
| **Wednesday** | Run | Easy (80%) | 4 miles easy run. Conversational pace, HR <150. Focus on cadence and form. | 40 min |
| **Thursday** | Run + Strength | Hard (20%) | **Run**: 10min easy warm-up + 6x800m hard (90%+ max HR, ~4:45 pace) with 3min easy jog recovery + 10min easy cool-down. **Then**: Strength B (see below) | 50 min run + 35 min strength |
| **Friday** | Swim | Easy (80%) | 800m easy swim: 200m warm-up + 4x100m freestyle + 4x50m kick + 200m cool-down. Mix of drills and steady pace. | 25 min |
| **Saturday** | Brick (Long) | Hard (20%) | **Bike**: 60min ride (20min easy warm-up + 30min at 80-85 RPM steady pace + 10min cool-down). **Run**: Immediately transition to 20min easy run off-bike to practice transitions. | 80 min total |
| **Sunday** | Active Recovery | Easy (80%) | 30min easy walk or very easy swim. Focus on moving, not training. Stretching and foam rolling. | 30 min |

## Strength A (Tuesday, after bike)

**Circuit format** (minimize rest between exercises):

| Exercise | Weight | Sets x Reps | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------------|-------|
| Incline Bench Press | 120 lbs | 3 x 8-10 | 1 RIR — stop when you could do 1 more rep |
| Cable Seated Row | 145 lbs | 3 x 11-13 | 1 RIR |
| Assisted Chin-ups | 70 lbs assist | 3 x 4-5 | 1 RIR |
| Dumbbell Lying Tricep Extension | 20 lbs | 3 x 10-12 | 1 RIR |

**Rest**: 60-90 seconds between exercises. Total time: ~35 min.

**Rationale**: Lighter session on Tuesday (hard bike day). Focus on maintaining strength, not building. Compound pulls + upper body only.

## Strength B (Thursday, after run)

**Full Body** (alternating upper/lower):

| Exercise | Weight | Sets x Reps | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------------|-------|
| Dumbbell Bench Press | 65 lbs | 3 x 10-12 | 1 RIR |
| Dumbbell Rows | 75 lbs | 3 x 12-14 | 1 RIR |
| Machine Leg Press | 330 lbs | 3 x 10-12 | 1 RIR |
| Lying Hamstring Curls | 130 lbs | 3 x 10-12 | 1 RIR |

**Rest**: 90-120 seconds between exercises. Total time: ~35 min.

**Rationale**: Thursday is hard run day, so strength is balanced (full body but controlled intensity).

## Intensity Targets by Zone

| Zone | HR Range | RPE | Pace/Effort | % of Workouts |
|------|----------|-----|------------|-----------------|
| **Easy (Z1-Z2)** | <75% max | 4-5/10 | Conversational | 80% |
| **Hard (Z4-Z5)** | 85-95% max | 8-10/10 | Race pace to threshold | 20% |
| **Transition (20-30s)** | Cooldown | 2-3/10 | Recovery | Between efforts |

## Weekly Targets

**Swim Volume**: 2,800m (3 sessions)
- Easy pace focus, build to 3-4km/week by W12

**Bike Volume**: 105 minutes (2 sessions)
- Threshold intervals + one long easy session
- Build to 5+ hours by W14

**Run Volume**: 14 miles (3 sessions)
- Threshold intervals + long brick
- Build to 20+ km by W14

**Strength**: 2x/week (maintenance)
- Upper body focus on Tuesday (lighter after hard bike)
- Full body on Thursday (balanced after hard run)
- Goal: Preserve strength, 1 RIR discipline

## Progression Plan (Next 4 Weeks)

| Week | Focus | Volume | Hard Workout Progression |
|------|-------|--------|-------------------------|
| W10 (this) | Establish baseline | ~10 hrs | 4x4min bike, 6x800m run |
| W11 | Volume increase | ~11 hrs | 5x4min bike OR 5x1000m run |
| W12 | Build intensity | ~12 hrs | 2x8min bike threshold, 3x1km run |
| W13 | Deload | ~8 hrs | Reduce volume 30%, maintain intensity |

## How to Log

**Every session, log:**
1. Swim: distance (meters), average pace, notes on form
2. Bike: duration (min), avg power (if available), HR zones
3. Run: distance (miles), average pace, HR zones
4. Strength: weight x reps for each exercise

**Weekly check-in:**
- Total volume (swim + bike run hours)
- Hard workout compliance (did you hit the targets?)
- Sleep quality + mood (recovery indicator)
- Any pain/aches?

## Notes

- **Strength is secondary** during triathlon build. Focus on hard cardio first.
- **Recovery is training**: Easy days are NOT rest days — they're low-intensity volume.
- **Heart rate matters**: Invest in a decent HR monitor (Garmin watch) to stay in zones.
- **Transitions matter**: Saturday brick teaches your legs to run hard off the bike.
- **Strength maintenance**: 1 RIR discipline prevents overtraining while preserving muscle.

## 16-Week Path to Half Ironman (Oct 2026)

- **W10-W13** (Mar-Apr): Base Building — establish aerobic foundation, introduce hard efforts
- **W14-W20** (May-Jun): Build Phase — increase volume, add race-pace work
- **W21-W23** (Jul-Aug): Peak Phase — long course simulation, taper begins
- **W24** (Sep): Taper — reduce volume 40-50%, maintain intensity
- **Race Week** (Oct): Final taper, race readiness

---

**Created**: 2026-03-02  
**Next Review**: 2026-03-09 (after first week of 80/20 protocol)

| **Wednesday** | Run | Easy (80%) | 4 miles easy run. Conversational pace, HR <150. Focus on cadence and form. | 40 min |
| **Thursday** | Run + Strength | Hard (20%) | **Run**: 10min easy warm-up + 6x800m hard (90%+ max HR, ~4:45 pace) with 3min easy jog recovery + 10min easy cool-down. **Then**: Strength B (see below) | 50 min run + 35 min strength |
| **Friday** | Swim | Easy (80%) | 800m easy swim: 200m warm-up + 4x100m freestyle + 4x50m kick + 200m cool-down. Mix of drills and steady pace. | 25 min |
| **Saturday** | Brick (Long) | Hard (20%) | **Bike**: 60min ride (20min easy warm-up + 30min at 80-85 RPM steady pace + 10min cool-down). **Run**: Immediately transition to 20min easy run off-bike to practice transitions. | 80 min total |
| **Sunday** | Active Recovery | Easy (80%) | 30min easy walk or very easy swim. Focus on moving, not training. Stretching and foam rolling. | 30 min |

## Strength A (Tuesday, after bike)

**Circuit format** (minimize rest between exercises):

| Exercise | Weight | Sets x Reps | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------------|-------|
| Incline Bench Press | 120 lbs | 3 x 8-10 | 1 RIR — stop when you could do 1 more rep |
| Cable Seated Row | 145 lbs | 3 x 11-13 | 1 RIR |
| Assisted Chin-ups | 70 lbs assist | 3 x 4-5 | 1 RIR |
| Dumbbell Lying Tricep Extension | 20 lbs | 3 x 10-12 | 1 RIR |

**Rest**: 60-90 seconds between exercises. Total time: ~35 min.

**Rationale**: Lighter session on Tuesday (hard bike day). Focus on maintaining strength, not building. Compound pulls + upper body only.

## Strength B (Thursday, after run)

**Full Body** (alternating upper/lower):

| Exercise | Weight | Sets x Reps | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------------|-------|
| Dumbbell Bench Press | 65 lbs | 3 x 10-12 | 1 RIR |
| Dumbbell Rows | 75 lbs | 3 x 12-14 | 1 RIR |
| Machine Leg Press | 330 lbs | 3 x 10-12 | 1 RIR |
| Lying Hamstring Curls | 130 lbs | 3 x 10-12 | 1 RIR |

**Rest**: 90-120 seconds between exercises. Total time: ~35 min.

**Rationale**: Thursday is hard run day, so strength is balanced (full body but controlled intensity).

## Intensity Targets by Zone

| Zone | HR Range | RPE | Pace/Effort | % of Workouts |
|------|----------|-----|------------|-----------------|
| **Easy (Z1-Z2)** | <75% max | 4-5/10 | Conversational | 80% |
| **Hard (Z4-Z5)** | 85-95% max | 8-10/10 | Race pace to threshold | 20% |
| **Transition (20-30s)** | Cooldown | 2-3/10 | Recovery | Between efforts |

## Weekly Targets

**Swim Volume**: 2,800m (3 sessions)
- Easy pace focus, build to 3-4km/week by W12

**Bike Volume**: 105 minutes (2 sessions)
- Threshold intervals + one long easy session
- Build to 5+ hours by W14

**Run Volume**: 14 miles (3 sessions)
- Threshold intervals + long brick
- Build to 20+ km by W14

**Strength**: 2x/week (maintenance)
- Upper body focus on Tuesday (lighter after hard bike)
- Full body on Thursday (balanced after hard run)
- Goal: Preserve strength, 1 RIR discipline

## Progression Plan (Next 4 Weeks)

| Week | Focus | Volume | Hard Workout Progression |
|------|-------|--------|-------------------------|
| W10 (this) | Establish baseline | ~10 hrs | 4x4min bike, 6x800m run |
| W11 | Volume increase | ~11 hrs | 5x4min bike OR 5x1000m run |
| W12 | Build intensity | ~12 hrs | 2x8min bike threshold, 3x1km run |
| W13 | Deload | ~8 hrs | Reduce volume 30%, maintain intensity |

## How to Log

**Every session, log:**
1. Swim: distance (meters), average pace, notes on form
2. Bike: duration (min), avg power (if available), HR zones
3. Run: distance (miles), average pace, HR zones
4. Strength: weight x reps for each exercise

**Weekly check-in:**
- Total volume (swim + bike run hours)
- Hard workout compliance (did you hit the targets?)
- Sleep quality + mood (recovery indicator)
- Any pain/aches?

## Notes

- **Strength is secondary** during triathlon build. Focus on hard cardio first.
- **Recovery is training**: Easy days are NOT rest days — they're low-intensity volume.
- **Heart rate matters**: Invest in a decent HR monitor (Garmin watch) to stay in zones.
- **Transitions matter**: Saturday brick teaches your legs to run hard off the bike.
- **Strength maintenance**: 1 RIR discipline prevents overtraining while preserving muscle.

## 16-Week Path to Half Ironman (Oct 2026)

- **W10-W13** (Mar-Apr): Base Building — establish aerobic foundation, introduce hard efforts
- **W14-W20** (May-Jun): Build Phase — increase volume, add race-pace work
- **W21-W23** (Jul-Aug): Peak Phase — long course simulation, taper begins
- **W24** (Sep): Taper — reduce volume 40-50%, maintain intensity
- **Race Week** (Oct): Final taper, race readiness

---

**Created**: 2026-03-02  
**Next Review**: 2026-03-09 (after first week of 80/20 protocol)

Protocol saved to `memory/protocol_2026-03-02_80-20.md` and ingested into your brain.

**Ready to start today?** Want me to log the first workout when you finish, or adjust anything?
What if I can’t swim 1000 meters straight?

Good catch — that's a real concern. Let me adjust:

**Option A: Broken sets (recommended for beginner swimmers)**
Instead of 1000m straight, do:
- 200m warm-up
- 6x100m easy (rest 20-30s between each)
- 200m cool-down
- **Total: 1000m, but broken into manageable chunks**

This is actually *better* for technique — you get recovery between reps, so form stays sharp.

**Option B: Reduce volume this week**
- Start with 600m total: 200m warm-up + 4x100m + 200m cool-down
- Build to 1000m by Week 12

**Option C: Mix modalities**
- 200m swim + 100m kick on board (counts as swim volume but easier)
- Reduces pure freestyle demand

**What's your current swim capacity?** 
- Can you do 100m continuous right now?
- How many reps before form breaks down?

Once I know, I'll scale the protocol to **your actual baseline** rather than a generic target. The 80/20 structure stays the same — we just adjust the distances.

Which option feels right?

1. Which workout (Monday swim, Tuesday bike, etc.)?
2. Distance/duration completed
3. How it felt (form, energy, any issues)
4. Heart rate data if you have it

Once you give me the details, I'll update your log and track it against the protocol.
